compiled_state.title: As a DSO orchestrator, check-usage.sh returns usage-aware throttle verdicts
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: New check-usage.sh script that polls the Anthropic OAuth usage endpoint, caches results in a shared flock-protected file, and returns a 3-tier throttle verdict via exit code
**Why**: Provides the usage signal that agent-batch-lifecycle.sh needs for dynamic throttle decisions, replacing the hard 5-agent cap
**Scope**:
- IN: OAuth polling, flock-protected caching (separate lockfile), CI fallback, curl timeout, cache TTL floor, 429 handling, exit code contract
- OUT: agent-batch-lifecycle.sh integration (Story 3), error-reactive fallback (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, check-usage.sh returns exit 0 (unlimited) when utilization is below throttle thresholds, exit 1 (throttled) at 90% 5hr / 95% 7day, and exit 2 (paused) at 95% 5hr / 98% 7day
  ← Satisfies: "check-usage.sh script polls the Anthropic OAuth usage endpoint, caches results in a shared flock-protected file"
- When this story is complete, cache is written to ~/.cache/claude/usage-cache.json (created via mkdir -p), locked via separate ~/.cache/claude/usage-cache.lock using Python fcntl.flock, with 5-min TTL and 60s minimum floor
  ← Satisfies: "caches results in a shared flock-protected file"
- When this story is complete, the cache contract is documented: JSON format with timestamp, five_hour_pct, seven_day_pct, resets_at fields; TTL semantics (stale = serve last verdict + attempt refresh)
  ← Satisfies: "caches results in a shared flock-protected file"
- When this story is complete, when no OAuth credentials are found (CI=true or absent file), check-usage.sh returns exit 0 with USAGE_SOURCE: no-credentials, bypassing all API and cache logic
  ← Satisfies: "check-usage.sh script polls the Anthropic OAuth usage endpoint"
- When this story is complete, curl --max-time 8 is enforced on all OAuth requests; on 429 with existing cache, last known verdict is served (fail-closed: stale unlimited degrades to exit 1/throttled to prevent optimism loop); on 429 with no cache, exit 1 (throttled) is returned
  ← Satisfies: "caches results in a shared flock-protected file"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Reliability] OAuth 429s are aggressive and persist for hours — 5-min cache + flock prevents multi-session hammering
- [Security] OAuth token values must never be logged; only log verdict and utilization percentages
- [Reliability] Python fcntl.flock for cross-platform portability (not flock(1) CLI)
- [Portability] Credential path discovery must handle multiple installation methods per spike findings
- [Reliability] Stale-cache-on-429 with unlimited verdict must degrade conservatively to prevent infinite optimism loop
